name: Convenience-store tea egg
default_safe_order: Good low-calorie protein add-on for a rushed meal.
why_stable: Tea eggs are durable chain-wide convenience-store staples.
why_risky: Calories are stable, but sodium is not trivial if stacked with processed foods.

name: Convenience-store roasted sweet potato
default_safe_order: Works well with a tea egg or chicken breast pack for a low-friction meal.
why_stable: Roasted sweet potato is a stable, durable convenience-store category across Taiwan chains.
why_risky: Weight changes the calories a lot, so the pack uses a range instead of a single number.

name: Convenience-store chicken breast pack
default_safe_order: Pair with sweet potato, salad cup, or unsweetened tea.
why_stable: Ready-to-eat chicken breast is a durable convenience-store protein family.
why_risky: Flavor-coated packs can push sodium and sugar above the plain baseline.

name: Convenience-store salad cup
default_safe_order: Use dressing as a separate calorie lane; grilled chicken salad is safer than creamy pasta salad.
why_stable: Cup salads are a durable convenience-store format even when exact SKUs rotate.
why_risky: Dressings and rich toppings change the final calories materially.

name: Convenience-store onigiri
default_safe_order: Fish or plain fillings are usually safer than mayo-heavy fillings.
why_stable: Rice balls are one of the most stable fresh-food families in Taiwan convenience stores.
why_risky: Mayo, fried, or double-carb fillings push the item out of the safer range.

name: Convenience-store sandwich
default_safe_order: Egg or lean-meat sandwiches with black coffee beat dessert bread plus sweet tea.
why_stable: Sandwich families are durable even when exact fillings rotate.
why_risky: Sweet bread, mayo, and combo drinks are the main drift drivers.

name: Convenience-store rice bowl or bento
default_safe_order: Prioritize grilled or braised proteins over creamy, fried, or curry-heavy boxes.
why_stable: Microwavable rice bowls and bentos are a durable category across chains.
why_risky: Rice weight, sauce, and side dishes drive a wide calorie spread.

name: Convenience-store pasta or noodles
default_safe_order: Tomato or clear-sauce pasta is usually safer than creamy or baked versions.
why_stable: Pasta trays are common across Taiwan convenience-store fresh-food hubs.
why_risky: Cream, cheese, and oil make exact calories highly item-dependent.

name: Convenience-store dessert cup
default_safe_order: Treat dessert as its own lane instead of pairing it automatically with a sweet drink.
why_stable: Dessert cups and bakery sweets are always present even when exact items rotate.
why_risky: Portions are small but sugar stacking is the main hidden problem.

name: Convenience-store sweet bottled tea or milk tea
default_safe_order: Swap to unsweetened tea, sparkling water, or black coffee when the meal already has starch.
why_stable: Sweet bottled drinks are a stable convenience-store trap category.
why_risky: Users often undercount liquid calories because the packaging feels small.
